title: dianthus petraeus subsp. stefanoffii
intro: Dianthus petraeus subsp. stefanoffii, also known as Mountain Pink, is a species of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. It is used in traditional medicine due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and calm digestive issues. The health benefits of Dianthus petraeus subsp. stefanoffii include its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, as well as its potential to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the flowers and leaves in hot water or a solvent like ethanol or glycerin.
